summary: Multi-language website with English as default, supporting Spanish, French, Portuguese, German, Turkish, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean, and Dutch
known_issues: Website displays error: 'useTranslationContext must be used within TranslationProvider' - translation context not properly initialized

existing_features.title: Wrap the application root in frontend/src/App.tsx with TranslationProvider (from frontend/src/contexts/TranslationContext.tsx) so that all components using useTranslationContext (via useTranslation hook) can access the translation context without throwing errors.

Add translations for Spanish, French, Portuguese, German, Turkish, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean, and Dutch to frontend/src/i18n/translations.ts alongside the existing English strings, covering all existing translation keys (nav, auth, home, discover, profile, social, settings, etc.).

Update frontend/src/contexts/TranslationContext.tsx to support all ten locales (en, es, fr, pt, de, tr, ru, vi, ko, nl), ensuring the translation function `t` resolves strings from the correct locale based on the user's language setting and falls back to English for missing keys.

Update the language selector in frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx to list all ten supported languages — English, Spanish, French, Portuguese, German, Turkish, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean, and Dutch — and persist the selected language via the existing useAccountSettings language mutation so it is reflected app-wide.

new_feature_requests.summary: Wrap the entire application root with TranslationProvider in frontend/src/main.tsx so that useTranslation hook can access the translation context throughout the component tree
Add multi-language support allowing users to switch the website language between English (default), Spanish, French, Portuguese, German, Turkish, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean, and Dutch

Update the backend language setting in backend/main.mo to accept and store all ten locale codes (en, es, fr, pt, de, tr, ru, vi, ko, nl) in UserSettings, replacing or extending any previous single-language field.
